grounding_quotes: This paper focuses on PPP-B2b, one of the featured services for BDS-3
The PPP-B2b signal, transmitted by BDS-3 GEO satellites, broadcasts precise orbit corrections, clock corrections and Differential Code Biases (DCB)
PPP is one of the featured services of BDS-3 for supporting high-precision positioning applications

experiment_epoch.date_description: PPP-B2b broadcast messages from August 1, 2020, to August 7, 2020 (DoY 214-220)
grounding_quotes: Evaluation is done using PPP-B2b broadcast messages from August 1, 2020, to August 7, 2020
during the period from August 1, 2020, to August 7, 2020

correction_types.reference_frame: ECEF, relative to satellite phase center

correction_types.reference: B3I signal for BDS, L1/L2 ionosphere-free for GPS

dcb_handling.description: PPP-B2b broadcasts DCB corrections for B1I, B1Cp, and B2ap signals relative to B3I. GPS DCBs NOT provided — users must use L1/L2 ionosphere-free combination for GPS.
dcb_handling.dcb_validation_reference: MGEX DCB products provided by CAS (Wang et al. 2016)
grounding_quotes: The differential code bias defines the transmitting delay difference of the signal components involved with respect to the satellite clock parameters
DCB(B1I) refers to the differential code bias between B1I and B3I
The GPS DCBs are not provided by the PPP-B2b signals and users are suggested to conduct L1/L2 dual-frequency GPS PPP
RMS of DCB(B1I) errors, DCB(B1Cp) errors and DCB(B2ap) errors are about 0.66 ns, 0.38 ns and 0.56 ns respectively

mathematical_model.processing_mode: Real-time PPP with dual-frequency ionosphere-free combination
mathematical_model.orbit_determination: Least-squares batch estimation with ISL + regional L-band measurements
mathematical_model.clock_estimation: Kalman filter, satellite clocks as white noise, 1ms/sqrt(1s) process noise

key_equations.name: ISL dual one-way measurement
key_equations.equation: rho_AB(t1) = |R_B(t1) - R_A(t1-dt1)| + c*clkB(t1) - c*clkA(t1) + c*tau_Send_A + c*tau_Rcv_B + delta_rho_AB_cor

grounding_quotes: A new orbit determination strategy of BDS-3 satellites is implemented for the PPP service, in which the BDS-3 orbits are estimated using inter-satellite link (ISL) measurements and observations from 7 stations in mainland China in combination
satellite clock estimation process is conducted in real-time with a Kalman filter

datasets.correction_source: PPP-B2b broadcast messages decoded by monitoring receiver at NUDT, Shanghai
datasets.orbit_determination_stations: 12 regional stations within mainland China + 30 globally-distributed stations (GPS only)
grounding_quotes: measurements from six International GNSS Monitoring and Assessment Service (iGMAS) stations in mainland China (bjf1, chu1, kun1, sha1, wuh1 and xia1)
The MGEX GFZ precise clock is taken as the reference for evaluating the accuracy of the PPP-B2b clock solutions

main_results.summary: PPP-B2b real-time positioning accuracy is at the same level as post-processed GFZ products. BDS-3/GPS dual-system PPP offers faster convergence and better accuracy than single-system.
key_findings: Orbit URE: 0.05m for BDS-3 MEO and GPS satellites; 0.15m for BDS-3 IGSO
Clock correction accuracy: ~0.2ns for BDS-3 and GPS satellites
BDS/GPS static positioning repeatability <1.0cm in horizontal, <1.5cm in vertical
PPP-B2b convergence: 7-12 min for static, <12 min for kinematic (BDS/GPS dual-system)
ISL measurements improve along-track and cross-track orbit accuracy due to better geometry
grounding_quotes: The positioning accuracy achievable using PPP-B2b real-time products is at the same level as that using post-processed products
The orbit URE is 0.05 m for the BDS-3 MEO and GPS satellites
The clock correction accuracy is 0.2 ns for BDS-3 and GPS satellites
The RMS for BDS-3/GPS dual-system static positioning errors is less than 1.0 cm in east and north and about 3.0 cm in the up

novelty_audit.rationale: Introduces a novel orbit determination strategy using ISL measurements + regional L-band code/phase measurements in combination, and a real-time Kalman filter clock estimation strategy for PPP-B2b correction generation. This is a significant engineering contribution that enables the PPP-B2b service itself.
novel_elements: ISL + regional L-band combined orbit determination for BDS-3
Real-time Kalman filter clock estimation with fixed predicted orbits
Comprehensive PPP-B2b correction generation pipeline description
grounding_quotes: A new orbit determination strategy using regional L-band code/phase measurements and inter-satellite link measurements in combination and a real-time clock estimation strategy based on the Kalman filter for PPP-B2b precise correction generation are introduced

reproduction_blockers: Customized receiver hardware needed to decode PPP-B2b messages from GEO satellites
Proprietary orbit/clock determination software (SHAO/BSNC) not publicly available
Regional monitoring network (12 stations in mainland China) not publicly accessible
Chinese-manufactured receiver antennas — PCO/PCV values not in igs14.atx
No source code or software release
PPP-B2b correction archive not publicly distributed
Real-time processing pipeline configuration not disclosed
